cli.server_feedback.preflight.docker_aut: Docker advice: this looks more like a registry authentication or permission issue. Log in to the registry and confirm the image is visible
cli.server_feedback.preflight.docker_bac: Docker advice: the current CLI path only wires up the docker CLI backend. Use --docker-backend cli or omit the argument
Docker advice: if you need future Engine API support, you may keep the record format, but the current version will not actually run engine_api
cli.server_feedback.preflight.docker_dae: Docker advice: confirm that Docker Desktop or Docker Engine is running and that the current user can access the daemon
cli.server_feedback.preflight.docker_dat: Docker advice: this looks like a missing container path mapping. Configure SEALANTERN_SERVERS_CONTAINER_ROOT / SEALANTERN_SERVERS_HOST_ROOT, or pass --data-dir explicitly
cli.server_feedback.preflight.docker_doc: Docker advice: run sealantern docker doctor first
cli.server_feedback.preflight.docker_ima: Docker advice: if the image is already cached locally, or you are using a private/offline image source, you can continue with the create/start/compose flow directly
Docker advice: the current docker_itzg runtime only accepts Minecraft server semantic images. Use itzg/minecraft-server, or keep your private image's final name as */minecraft-server
Docker advice: this looks more like an image name or tag error. Check whether --image / --image-tag exists
Docker advice: if the image is not cached locally and later needs to be pulled over the network, run sealantern docker pull {0} after the network recovers
Docker advice: the current image is not suitable for --command-mode docker_stdio. If there is no strong dependency, switch back to --command-mode rcon first
Docker advice: if you insist on docker_stdio, confirm the image provides mc-send-to-console and follows the itzg CREATE_CONSOLE_IN_PIPE semantics
cli.server_feedback.preflight.docker_net: Docker advice: this looks more like a network or proxy issue. Check registry connectivity, proxy settings, and the TLS/certificate environment
cli.server_feedback.preflight.docker_pul: Docker advice: if you need to validate image availability over the network, run sealantern docker pull {0}
cli.server_feedback.preflight.docker_ret: Docker advice: after the environment recovers, retry sealantern server {0} --runtime docker ...
cli.server_feedback.preflight.docker_tim: Docker advice: if the network is slow or the registry is unstable, retry later, or inspect the host environment with sealantern docker doctor first
Docker advice: the image probe timed out, and the CLI will continue as a soft failure. If the host does not already have the image, warm it up with docker pull after the network recovers
cli.server_feedback.preflight.local_java: Local advice: you are currently using --J, which only resolves Java from JAVA_HOME/PATH. If the environment is not configured, switch to an explicit --java path
Local advice: after fixing Java, retry sealantern server start {0} or rerun the create flow
Local advice: if neither the environment variables nor the default config matched, the CLI will prompt whether to run a global Java scan
Local advice: specify --java <path> explicitly, or use --java %env:JAVA_HOME% / --java %env:Path%
cli.server_feedback.preflight.review_run: You can continue with: sealantern server help    # review {0} runtime arguments
